notes: New skill: 100% pass rate (25/25 assertions). Old skill: 96% (24/25). Only failure: C6 (--confirm on batch add-source).
Token usage: new skill averages 28.7K tokens vs old skill 32.8K — 12.5% reduction per eval despite 73% smaller skill file (191 vs 702 lines).

Eval 1 and Eval 2 are non-discriminating on pass rate — both skills pass all assertions. However, the old skill agent explicitly noted focus prompts were 'crafted from scratch, not from skill guidance', while new skill's focus prompts directly derive from workflow recipes.

Eval 3 is the only discriminating eval: old skill omits --confirm on batch add-source (C6 fail), and provides batch studio WITHOUT --difficulty (documentation gap noted by agent). New skill includes --difficulty in batch command.

The real value of the new skill is not captured by binary assertions: (1) clinical focus prompt templates guide non-medical-expert agents, (2) Windows cp1252 workaround, (3) correct MCP prefix (mcp__notebooklm__* vs wrong mcp__notebooklm-mcp__*), (4) 73% size reduction saves tokens at scale.
